item.title: ZMC Group Recalls LED Finger Beam Lights Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Battery Ingestion; Violates Mandatory Standard for Toys
item.company: Various discount stores nationwide from August 2025 through March 2026 for about $1.
item.brand: Various discount stores nationwide from August 2025 through March 2026 for about $1.
item.summary: This recall involves LED Finger Beam Lights, model A10-8. The toy comes packaged in a hand-shaped cardboard back with four colors (red, white, blue and green) of light. Each light contains three pre-installed AG3 button cell batteries. The back of the packaging has the model number "A10-8", identification of the distributor and manufacturer, production date and batch number printed on a sticker label.
item.affectedPeople: Consumers who purchased: LED Finger Beam Lights.
item.remedy: Consumers should take the LED Finger Beam Lights away from children, stop using them immediately and contact ZMC Group for full refund or a replacement product. Consumers should remove the batteries, break each component in half and submit a photo of the destroyed pieces to recallzmctoy@gmail.com. Note: Button cell batteries are hazardous. Batteries should be disposed of or recycled by following local hazardous waste procedures.
item.payoutAmount: Repair, refund, replacement, or other remedy if stated by the CPSC notice
